summary:
The statement that "tocilizumab is an anti-TNF antibody" is false because tocilizumab specifically targets interleukin-6 (IL-6) rather than tumor necrosis factor alpha (TNF). It belongs to a different class of biologic therapies used for autoimmune diseases but lacks activity against TNF pathways.

The claim that tocilizumab is an anti-TNF antibody is false; instead, it inhibits interleukin-6 (IL-6), which makes it distinct from true anti-TNF therapeutics. This distinction highlights important differences between classes of biologicals based on their target cytokine mechanisms. 

Final Answer: No

Only summarize the core clinical point(s).

No — tocilizumab is not an anti-TNF antibody; it blocks interleukin-6 (IL-6), not tumor necrosis factor-alpha (TNF). Its mechanism of action distinguishes it from traditional anti-TNF therapies.  

(End summary)

We are only asked to provide two concise sentences focusing solely on the **key clinical points**, so we must distill this down strictly:

Tocilizumab targets interleukin-6 (IL-6), not tumor necrosis factor-alpha (TNF). Therefore, it is incorrectly classified as an anti-TNF antibody.
